author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LXXXVII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Psalm which has just been sung is short, if we look to the number of its words, but of deep interest in its thoughts.…The subject of song and praise in that Psalm is a city, whose citizens are we, as far as we are Christians: whence we are absent, as long as we are mortal: whither we are tending: through whose approaches, undiscoverable among the brakes and thorns that entangle them, the Sovereign of the city made Himself a path for us to reach it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Walking thus in Christ, and pilgrims till we arrive, and sighing as we long for a certain ineffable repose that dwells within that city, a repose of which it is promised, that “the eye of man hath never seen” such, “nor ear heard, nor hath it entered into his heart to conceive;” let us chant the song of a longing heart: for he who truly longs, thus sings within his soul, though his tongue be silent: he who does not, however he may resound in human ears, is voiceless to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See what ardent lovers of that city were they by whom these words were composed, by whom they have been handed down to us; with how deep a feeling were they sung by those!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A feeling that the love of that city created in them: that love the Spirit of God inspired; “the love of God,” he saith, “shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Ghost, which is given unto us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fervent with this Spirit then, let us listen to what is said of that city.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Her foundations are upon the holy hills” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Psalm had as yet said nothing of the city: it begins thus, and says, “Her foundations are upon the holy hills.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There can be no doubt that foundations, especially among the hills, belong to some city.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus filled with the Holy Spirit, and with many thoughts of love and longing for that city, as if after long internal meditation, that citizen bursts out, “Her foundations are upon the holy hills;” as if he had already said something concerning it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how could he have said nothing on a subject, respecting which in his heart he had never been silent?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how could “her foundations” have been written, of which nothing had been said before?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, as I said, after long and silent travailing in contemplation of that city in his mind, crying to God, he bursts out into the ears of men thus: “Her foundations are upon the holy hills.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And, supposing persons who heard to enquire of what city he spoke he adds, “the Lord loveth the gates of Sion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, then, a city whose foundations are upon the holy hills, a city called Sion, whose gates the Lord loveth, as he adds, “above all the dwellings of Jacob.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what doth this mean, “her foundations on the holy hills”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the holy hills upon which this city is built?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Another citizen tells us this more explicitly, the Apostle Paul: of this was the Prophet a citizen, of this the Apostle citizen: and they spoke to exhort the other citizens.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how are these, I mean the Prophets and Apostles, citizens?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps in this sense; that they are themselves the hills, upon which are the foundations of this city, whose gates the Lord loveth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let then another citizen state this clearly, that I may not seem to guess.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Speaking to the Gentiles, and telling them how they were returning, and being, as it were, framed together into the holy structure, “built,” he says, “upon the foundations of the Apostles and Prophets:” and because neither the Apostles nor Prophets, upon whom the foundations of that city rest, could stand by their own power, he adds, “Jesus Christ Himself being the head comer stone.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That the Gentiles, therefore, might not think they had no relation to Sion: for Sion was a certain city of this world, which bore a typical resemblance as a shadow to that Sion of which he presently speaketh, that Heavenly Jerusalem, of which the Apostle saith, “which is the mother of us all;” they might not be said to bear no relation to Sion, on the ground that they did not belong to the Jewish people, he addresses them thus: “Now therefore ye are no more strangers and foreigners, but fellow citizens with the saints, and of the household of God, and are built upon the foundation of the Apostles and Prophets.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou seest the structure of so great a city: yet whereon does all that edifice repose, where does it rest, that it may never fall?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Jesus Christ Himself,” he saith, “being the head corner stone.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 3. …But that ye may know that Christ is at once the earliest and the highest foundation, the Apostle saith, “Other foundation can no man lay than is laid, which is Christ Jesus.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How, then, are the Prophets and Apostles foundations, and yet Christ so, than whom nothing can be higher?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How, think you, save that as He is openly styled, Saint of saints, so figuratively Foundation of foundations?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus if thou art thinking of mysteries, Christ is the Saint of saints: if of a subject flock, the Shepherd of shepherds: if of a structure, the Pillar of pillars.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In material edifices, the same stone cannot be above and below: if at the bottom, it cannot be at the top: and vice versa: for almost all bodies are liable to limitations in space: nor can they be everywhere or for ever; but as the Godhead is in every place, from every place symbols may be taken for It; and not being any of these things in external properties, It can be everything in figure.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is Christ a door, in the same sense as the doors we see made by carpenters?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Surely not; and yet He said, “I am the door.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or a shepherd, in the same capacity as those who guard sheep? though He said, “I am the Shepherd.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Both these names occur in the same passage: in the Gospel, He said, that the shepherd enters by the door: the words are, “I am the good Shepherd;” and in the same passage, “I am the door:” and who is the shepherd who enters by the door?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I am the good Shepherd:” and what is the door by which Thou, Good Shepherd, enterest?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then art Thou all things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the sense in which everything is through Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To explain: when Paul enters by the door, does not Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not because Paul is Christ: but since Christ is in Paul: and Paul acts through Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostle says, “Do ye seek a proof of Christ speaking in me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When His saints and faithful disciples enter by the door, does not Christ enter by the door?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How are we to prove this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since Saul, not yet called Paul, was persecuting those very saints, when He called to him from Heaven, “Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Himself then is the foundation, and corner stone: rising from the bottom: if indeed from the bottom: for the base of this foundation is the highest exaltation of the building: and as the support of bodily fabrics rests upon the ground, that of spiritual structures reposes on high.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were we building up ourselves upon the earth, we should lay our foundation on the lowest level: but since our edifice is a heavenly one, to Heaven our Foundation has gone before us: so that our Saviour, the corner stone, the Apostles, and mighty Prophets, the hills that bear the fabric of the city, constitute a sort of living structure.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This building now cries from your hearts; that you may be built up into its fabric, the hand of God, as of an artificer, worketh even through my tongue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor was it without a meaning that Noah’s ark was made of “square beams,” which were typical of the form of the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what is it to be made square?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen to the resemblance of the squared stone: like qualities should the Christian have: for in all his trials he never falls: though pushed, and, as it were, turned over, he falls not: and thus too, whichever way a square stone is turned, it stands erect.…In earthly cities, one thing is the structure of buildings: another thing are the citizens that dwell therein: that city is builded of its own inmates, who are themselves the blocks that form the city, for the very stones are living: “Ye also,” says the Apostle, “as living stones, are built up a spiritual house,” words that are addressed to ourselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us then pursue the contemplation of that city.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord loveth the gates of Sion more than all the dwellings of Jacob” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have made the foregoing remarks, that ye may not imagine the gates are one thing, the foundations another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why are the Apostles and Prophets foundations?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because their authority is the support of our weakness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why are they gates? because through them we enter the kingdom of God: for they proclaim it to us: and while we enter by their means, we enter also through Christ, Himself being the Gate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And twelve gates of Jerusalem are spoken of, and the one gate is Christ, and the twelve gates are Christ for Christ dwells in the twelve gates, hence was twelve the number of the Apostles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a deep mystery in this number of twelve: “Ye shall sit,” says our Saviour, “on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If there are twelve thrones there, there will be no room for the judgment-seat of Paul, the thirteenth Apostle, though he says that he shall judge not men only, but even Angels; which, but the fallen Angels?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Know ye not, that we shall judge Angels,” he writes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The world would answer, Why dost thou boast that thou shalt be a judge?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where will be thy throne?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Lord spoke of twelve thrones for the twelve Apostles: one, Judas, fell, and his place being supplied by Matthias, the number of twelve thrones was made up: first, then, discover room for thy judgment-seat; then threaten that thou wilt judge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us, therefore, reflect upon the meaning of the twelve thrones.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The expression is typical of a sort of universality, as the Church was destined to prevail throughout the whole world: whence this edifice is styled a building together into Christ: and because judges come from all quarters, the twelve thrones are spoken of, just as the twelve gates, from the entering in from all sides into that city.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not only therefore have those twelve, and the Apostle Paul, a claim to the twelve thrones, but, from the universal signification, all who are to sit in judgment: in the same manner as all who enter the city, enter by one or the other of the twelve gates.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are four quarters of the globe: East, West, North, and South: and they are constantly alluded to in the Scriptures.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From all those four winds; our Lord declares in the Gospel that He will call his sheep “from the four winds;” therefore from all those four winds is the Church called.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how called?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On every side it is called in the Trinity: no otherwise is it called than by Baptism in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Ghost: four then being thrice taken, twelve are found.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Knock, therefore, with all your hearts at these gates: and let Christ cry within you: “Open me the gates of righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He went before us the Head: He follows Himself in His Body.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Very excellent things are said of thee, thou city of God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was, as it were, contemplating that city of Jerusalem on earth: for consider what city he alludes to, of which certain very excellent things are spoken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now the earthly city has been destroyed: after suffering the enemy’s rage, it fell to the earth; it is no longer what it was: it exhibited the emblem, and the shadow hath passed away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence then are “very excellent things spoken of thee, thou city of God”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen whence: “I will think upon Rahab and Babylon, with them that know Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that city, the Prophet, in the person of God, says, “I will think upon Rahab and Babylon.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rahab belongs not to the Jewish people; Babylon belongs not to the Jewish people; as is clear from the next verse: “For the Philistines also, and Tyre, with the Ethiopians, were there.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deservedly then, “very excellent things are spoken of thee, thou city of God:” for not only is the Jewish nation, born of the flesh of Abraham, included therein, but all nations also, some of which are named that all may be understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will think,” he says, “upon Rahab:” who is that harlot?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That harlot in Jericho, who received the spies and conducted them out of the city by a different road: who trusted beforehand in the promise, who feared God, who was told to hang out of the window a line of scarlet thread, that is, to bear upon her forehead the sign of the blood of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She was saved there, and thus represented the Church of the Gentiles: whence our Lord said to the haughty Pharisees, “Verily I say unto you, that the publicans and the harlots go into the kingdom of God before you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They go before, because they do violence: they push their way by faith, and to faith a way is made, nor can any resist, since they who are violent take it by force.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is written, “The kingdom of Heaven suffereth violence, and the violent take it by force.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such was the conduct of the robber, more courageous on the cross than in the place of ambush.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will think upon Rahab and Babylon.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By Babylon is meant the city of this world: as there is one holy city, Jerusalem; one unholy, Babylon: all the unholy belong to Babylon, even as all the holy to Jerusalem.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he slideth from Babylon to Jerusalem.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How, but by Him who justifieth the ungodly: Jerusalem is the city of the saints; Babylon of the wicked: but He cometh who justifieth the ungodly: since it is said, “I will think” not only “upon Rahab,” but “upon Babylon,” but with whom? “with them that know Me.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen now to a deep mystery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rahab is there through Him, through whom also is Babylon, now no longer Babylon, but beginning to be Jerusalem.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The daughter is divided against her mother, and will be among the members of that queen to whom is said, “Forget thine own people, and thy father’s house, so shall the king have pleasure in thy beauty.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how could Babylon aspire to Jerusalem?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How could Rahab reach those foundations?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How could the Philistines, or Tyre, or the people of the Ethiopians?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen to this verse, “Sion, my mother, a man shall say.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is then a man who saith this: through whom all those I have mentioned make their approach.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is this man?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It tells if we hear, if we understand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It follows, as if a question had been raised, through whose aid Rahab, Babylon, the Philistines, Tyre, and the Morians, gained an entrance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, through whom they come; “Sion, my mother, a man shall say; and a man was born in her, and Himself the Most High hath founded her” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What, my brethren, can be clearer?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly, because “very excellent things are spoken of thee, thou city of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, “Sion, O mother, a man shall say.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He who was born in her.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is then the man who was born in her, and He Himself hath founded her.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet how can He be born in the city which He Himself founded?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It had already been founded, that therein He might be born.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Understand it thus, if thou canst: “Mother Sion, he shall say;” but it is “a man” that “shall say, Mother Sion; yea, a man was born in her:” and yet “he hath founded her” (not a man, but), “the Most High.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As He created a mother of whom He would be born, so He founded a city in which He would be born.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hope is ours, brethren!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On our behalf the Most High, who founded the city, addresses that city as a mother: and “He was born in her, and the Most High hath founded her.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As though it were said, How do ye know this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All of us have sung these Psalms: and Christ, Man for our sake, God before us, sings within us all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But is this much to say, “before us,” of Him who was before heaven and earth and time?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then, born for our sakes a man, in that city, also founded her when He was the Most High.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet how are we assured of this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord shall rehearse it when He writeth up the people” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6), as the following verse has it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord shall declare, when He writeth up the people, and their princes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What princes?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Those who were born in her;” those princes who, born within her walls, became therein princes: for before they could become princes in her, God chose the despised things of the world to confound the strong.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was the fisherman, the publican, a prince?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were indeed princes: but because they became such in her.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Princes of what kind were they?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Princes come from Babylon, believing monarchs of this world, came to the city of Rome, as to the head of Babylon: they went not to the temple of the Emperor, but to the tomb of the Fisherman.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence indeed did they rank as princes?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God chose the weak things of the world to confound the strong, and the foolish things He hath chosen, and things which are not as though they were, that things which are may be brought to nought.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This He doth who “from the ground raises the helpless, and from the dunghill exalts the poor.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what purpose?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That He may set him with the princes, even with the princes of His people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is a mighty deed, a deep source of pleasure and exultation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Orators came later into that city, but they could never have done so, had not fishermen preceded them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These things are glorious indeed, but where could they take place, but in that city of God, of whom very excellent things are spoken?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So thus, after drawing together and mingling every source of joyous exultation, how doth he conclude?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The dwelling as of all that shall be made joyous is in Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if all made joyous, all rejoicing, shall dwell in that city.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Amid our journeyings here we suffer bruises: our last home shall be the home of joy alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Toil and groans shall perish: prayers pass away, hymns of praise succeed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There shall be the dwelling of the happy; no longer shall there be the groans of those that long, but the gladness of those who enjoy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He will be present for whom we sigh: we shall be like Him, as we shall see Him as He is: there it will be our whole task to praise and enjoy the presence of God: and what beyond shall we ask for, when He alone satisfies us, by whom all things were made?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We shall dwell and be dwelt in; and shall be subject to Him, that God may be all in all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed,” then, “are they that dwell in Thy house.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How blessed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Blessed in their gold, and silver, their numerous slaves, and multiplied offspring?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed are they that dwell in Thy house: for ever and ever they will be praising Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Blessed in that sole labour which is rest!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let this then be the one and only object of our desire, my brethren, when we shall have reached this pass.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let, us prepare ourselves to rejoice in God: to praise Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The good works which conduct us thither, will not be needed there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I described, as far as I could, only yesterday, our condition there: works of charity there will be none, where there will be no misery: thou shalt not find one in want, one naked, no one will meet you tormented with thirst, there will be no stranger, no sick to visit, no dead to bury, no disputants to set at peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then wilt thou find to do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall we plant new vines, plough, traffic, make voyages, to support the necessities of the body?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deep quiet shall be there; all toilsome work, that necessity demands, will cease: the necessity being dead, its works will perish too.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then will be our state?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As far as possible, the tongue of a man thus told us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As it were, the dwelling of all who shall be made perfect is in Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why does he say, “as it were”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because there shall be such joy there as we know not here.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many pleasures do I behold here, and many rejoice in this world, some in one thing, others in another; but there is nothing to compare with that delight, but it shall be “as it were” being made joyful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if I say joyfulness, men at once think of such joyfulness as men use to have in wine, in feasting, in avarice, and in the world’s distinctions.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For men are elated by these things, and mad with a kind of joy: but “there is no joy, saith the Lord, unto the wicked.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a sort of joyfulness which the ear of man hath not heard, nor his eye seen, nor hath it entered into his heart to conceive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As it were, the dwelling of all who shall be made joyful is in Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us prepare for other delights: for a kind of shadow is what we find here, not the reality: that we may not expect to enjoy such things there as here we delight in: otherwise our self-denial will be avarice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some persons, when invited to a rich banquet, where there are many and costly dishes yet to come on, abstain from breaking their fast: if you ask the reason, they tell you that they are fasting: which is indeed a great work, a Christian work.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet be not hasty in praising them: examine their motives: it is their belly, not religion, that they are consulting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That their appetite may not be palled by ordinary dishes, they abstain till more delicate food is set before them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This fast then is for the gullet’s sake.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fasting is undoubtedly important: it fights against the belly and the palate; but sometimes it fights for them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus, my brethren, if ye imagine that we shall find any such pleasures in that country to which the heavenly trumpet urges us on, and on that account abstain from present enjoyments, that ye may receive the like more plentifully there, ye imitate those I have described, who fast only for greater feasting, and abstain only for greater indulgence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not ye like this: prepare yourselves for a certain ineffable delight: cleanse your hearts from all earthly and secular affections.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We shall see something, the sight of which will make us blessed: and that alone will suffice for us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall we not eat?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yes: we shall eat: but that shall be our food, which will ever refresh, and never fail.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In Thee is the dwelling of all who shall be, as it were, made joyful.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He has already told us how we shall be made joyful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed are they that dwell in thy house: for ever and ever they will be praising Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us praise the Lord as far as we are able, but with mingled lamentations: for while we praise we long for Him, and as yet have Him not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When we have, all our sorrows will be taken from us, and nothing will remain but praise, unmixed and everlasting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now let us pray.
